annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

tools.description: Close a GitHub issue with a closing comment. Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.

body.description: Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

state_reason.description: The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.

tools.name: close_issue

tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: remove_labels

tools.description: Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.

assignee.description: Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: assign_to_user

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization

message.description: The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols

owners.description: Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')

tools.name: mention_owners

content.text: {"number":58187,"title":"[Identity] Docs: Azure.Identity.Broker README outdated after 1.6.0 release","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.Identity.Broker` **Service directory:** `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity.Broker/` **Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (version increment after release of Azure.Identity.Broker 1.6.0) by `@azure-sdk` ## What Changed Azure.Identity.Broker 1.6.0 was released on 2026-04-14 with a critical dependency change: the package now depends on `Azure.Core` directly (not `Azure.Identity`), as the Azure.Identity types moved to Azure.Core in the 1.53.0 type consolidation. Additionally, several features added across versions 1.3.0–1.6.0 remain undocumented in the README. ## Gaps Found - **Outdated Prerequisites section**: Still lists `Azure.Identity` as a prerequisite, but since 1.6.0 the package depends on `Azure.Core` directly. The README should also call out the critical compatibility requirement: version 1.6.0+ is required when using `Azure.Core` 1.53.0+ (or `Azure.Identity` 1.21.0+), with a link to the [migration guide](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/MigrationGuide.md#azureidentitybroker-compatibility). - **Missing "Examples" section**: The README has no "Examples" section, which is required by the standard README template. There are no sample test files under `tests/Samples/` either. Concrete code examples for creating `InteractiveBrowserCredential` with a parent window handle (HWND) are needed. - **Cross-platform support not documented**: Version 1.3.0 (stable) added macOS and Linux/WSL broker support, but the README only mentions Windows (WAM) implicitly. Platform requirements and limitations should be documented. - **DI/Configuration integration undocumented**: Version 1.4.0 added experimental `Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration` and `Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection` integration (with link to configuration docs), but the README doesn't mention it. - **JSON schema / appsettings.json IntelliSense undocumented**: Version 1.5.0 added a JSON schema segment enabling IntelliSense and validation for credential configuration in `appsettings.json`, but the README doesn't mention it. - **No `samples/` directory**: Unlike comparable packages, there are no standalone sample files for this package. 📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **Edit** `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity.Broker/README.md`: 1. **Update Prerequisites section** — replace the existing `Azure.Identity` prerequisite entry: ```markdown ### Prerequisites * An [Azure subscription][azure_sub]. * The [Azure.Core][azure_core] package (1.53.0 or later, included transitively via `Azure.Identity` 1.21.0+). > **Important:** Azure.Identity.Broker 1.6.0 or later is required when your application references `Azure.Core` 1.53.0+ or `Azure.Identity` 1.21.0+. Earlier versions of this package are incompatible with the type consolidation introduced in those releases. See the [migration guide][broker_migration] for details. ``` Update the link references at the bottom of the README to add: ```markdown [azure_core]: https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/tree/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md [broker_migration]: https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/MigrationGuide.md#azureidentitybroker-compatibility ``` 2. **Add "Examples" section** — insert between the "Key concepts" and "Troubleshooting" sections: ```markdown ## Examples ### Authenticate with InteractiveBrowserCredential using a broker ``` ``` ``` The snippet content will be filled in by `Update-Snippets.ps1` after writing the sample test file in Step 2. 3. **Add cross-platform support note** in the "Key concepts" section: ```markdown ### Platform support The authentication broker is supported on: - **Windows**: Windows Authentication Manager (WAM) via the built-in broker. - **macOS**: Microsoft broker (added in 1.3.0). - **Linux and WSL**: Microsoft broker (added in 1.3.0). Prerequisites and setup instructions are described in [Enable SSO in native Linux apps using MSAL.NET]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) ``` 4. **Add DI/Configuration integration note** in the "Key concepts" section: ```markdown ### Configuration and dependency injection This package supports optional integration with `Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration` and `Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection`. For details, see the [Configuration and Dependency Injection](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/src/docs/ConfigurationAndDependencyInjection.md) documentation. A JSON schema is included in the NuGet package to provide IntelliSense and validation for credential configuration in `appsettings.json`. ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets **Create** `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity.Broker/tests/Samples/Sample1_InteractiveBroker.cs`: ```csharp // Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved. // Licensed under the MIT License. using System; using Azure.Identity; using Azure.Identity.Broker; using Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets; using NUnit.Framework; namespace Azure.Identity.Broker.Tests.Samples { public class Sample1_InteractiveBroker { [Test] [Ignore("This test is a live test that requires user interaction.")] public void InteractiveBrowserWithBroker() { #region Snippet:Identity_Broker_InteractiveBrowser // Obtain the parent window handle for your application. // On a WinForms or WPF application, use the handle of the main form/window. // The value IntPtr.Zero instructs the broker to select an appropriate parent window automatically. IntPtr parentWindowHandle = IntPtr.Zero; var credential = new InteractiveBrowserCredential( new InteractiveBrowserCredentialBrokerOptions(parentWindowHandle)); var client = new SecretClient(new Uri("(myvault.vault.azure.net/redacted), credential); #endregion } [Test] [Ignore("This test is a live test that requires user interaction.")] public void InteractiveBrowserWithBrokerUseDefaultAccount() { #region Snippet:Identity_Broker_UseDefaultBrokerAccount // Set UseDefaultBrokerAccount to true to silently use the currently signed-in // operating system account, bypassing the interactive sign-in prompt. IntPtr parentWindowHandle = IntPtr.Zero; var options = new InteractiveBrowserCredentialBrokerOptions(parentWindowHandle) { UseDefaultBrokerAccount = true }; var credential = new InteractiveBrowserCredential(options); #endregion } } } ``` - In the README, insert the following placeholder in the new "Examples" section for the first snippet: ````markdown ```C# Snippet:Identity_Broker_InteractiveBrowser ``` ```` And a second example for default account: ````markdown ### Authenticate with the signed-in OS account (silent) ```C# Snippet:Identity_Broker_UseDefaultBrokerAccount ``` ```` ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity.Broker/README.md` currently has these sections: | # | Section | Status | |---|---------|--------| | 1 | Getting started (Install, Prerequisites, Authenticate) | ⚠️ Prerequisites outdated | | 2 | Key concepts | ⚠️ Missing platform support and DI/config notes | | 3 | Examples | ❌ Missing entirely | | 4 | Troubleshooting | ✅ Present | | 5 | Next steps | ✅ Present | | 6 | Contributing | ✅ Present | Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next: 1. `dotnet build sdk/identity/Azure.Identity.Broker/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/identity/Azure.Identity.Broker/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 identity` — this reads `#region Snippet:` tags from test files and injects the code into README.md placeholder blocks. Check the git diff after running to confirm README was updated correctly. 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 identity` — regenerates public API listing files. Required if any public types or members were added or changed. 5. `dotnet format sdk/identity/Azure.Identity.Broker/` — ensures consistent code formatting. ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above. > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24480182032/agentic_workflow) · ● 401.2K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58187","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-15T22:02:25Z","updated_at":"2026-04-15T22:02:25Z"}

{"total_count":2,"incomplete_results":false,"items":[{"id":3001824349,"number":49459,"state":"closed","state_reason":"completed","locked":true,"title":"[BUG] `InteractiveBrowserCredentialBrokerOptions.UseDefaultBrokerAccount` doesn't do what it says","body":"### Library name and version

Azure.Identity 1.14.0-beta.3 / Azure.Identity.Broker 1.3.0-beta.2

### Describe the bug

The docs for `UseDefaultBrokerAccount ` state 

https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/[REDACTED]/sdk/identity/Azure.Identity.Broker/src/InteractiveBrowserCredentialBrokerOptions.cs#L23-L26

I interpreted this to mean that I shouldn't expect to see the account selection window.

### Expected behavior

I expect to not see the account selection screen, and to be taken straight to the next screen - in my case an MFA prompt.

Alternatively if this is the expected behaviour from WAM side, I expect the documentation for `UseDefaultBrokerAccount` to be clarified.

### Actual behavior

I get a prompt asking me to select an account.  This selection includes a single option which is my windows connected account.  (The redacted value is my account's UPN).
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/62058279-7f9d-42a8-a8c2-ce71f54a02d5)

### Reproduction Steps

```cs
new InteractiveBrowserCredential(new InteractiveBrowserCredentialBrokerOptions(IntPtr.Zero)
        {
            UseDefaultBrokerAccount = true,
        })
```

Brokered Credential spam logs","body":"### Library name and version

Azure.Identity 1.14.0-beta.3 / Azure.Identity.Broker 1.3.0-beta.2

### Describe the bug

If you forward logs to `ILogger` through `AzureEventSourceLogForwarder`, the getting a token through the WAM broker outputs nearly 200 lines of logs!  This seems a bit excessive.

### Expected behavior

Much of the output logs look like debug level, and are also spread out unnesscarially over several log entries.  I'd expect many of these logs to be at debug level, or for some of the log entries to be combined into one single entry rather than spread out into indivdiual log entries (see `LogTelemetryData` logs for many examples that could be combined).

### Reproduction Steps

Use the following code, ensuring that you credentials are resolved through Brokered Credentials, not an earlier type in the chain:

```cs
var logger = loggerFactory.CreateLogger<CredentialProvider>();
using var forwarder = new AzureEventSourceLogForwarder(loggerFactory);
forwarder.Start();
var credential = new DefaultAzureCredential(new DefaultAzureCredentialOptions()
{
    // Managed Identity Credentials are slow, and not typically used in docker scenarios
    ExcludeManagedIdentityCredential = true,
    ExcludeAzureCliCredential = true
});
var token = await credential.GetTokenAsync(new(scopes: ["https://management.azure.com/.default"]));
```

## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This bot-filed issue identifies documentation gaps in the `Azure.Identity.Broker` README that need to be addressed following the 1.6.0 release, including an outdated prerequisites section, missing Examples section, undocumented cross-platform support, and missing DI/Configuration integration notes.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.Identity.Broker` (1.6.0)
**Affected API:** `InteractiveBrowserCredential`, `InteractiveBrowserCredentialBrokerOptions`
**Scenarios:**
- Outdated Prerequisites section still references `Azure.Identity` instead of `Azure.Core` (changed in 1.6.0 due to type consolidation)
- Missing "Examples" section required by standard README template, with no sample test files
- Cross-platform broker support (macOS, Linux/WSL added in 1.3.0) not documented
- DI/Configuration integration added in 1.4.0 not mentioned in README
- JSON schema / appsettings.json IntelliSense added in 1.5.0 not mentioned in README
- No `samples/` directory unlike comparable packages

**Root ask:** Update `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity.Broker/README.md` with accurate prerequisites, add an Examples section with code snippets, document platform support, and add DI/Config integration notes.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This is a documentation-only issue generated by an automated workflow after the 1.6.0 release of `Azure.Identity.Broker`. No code bug or runtime failure is involved — the gaps are purely in the README and missing sample files.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Review `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity.Broker/README.md` to confirm the documented gaps are accurate against the current file state.
2. Create `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity.Broker/tests/Samples/Sample1_InteractiveBroker.cs` with `#region Snippet:` markers, then run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 identity` to inject snippets into the README.
3. Verify the build and tests still pass: `dotnet build sdk/identity/Azure.Identity.Broker/` and `dotnet test sdk/identity/Azure.Identity.Broker/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`.
